description: Remove mock inference fallback from LlamaCppEngineImpl and fail clearly when native libraries are not available. Also clean up broken symlinks in JniLibs (uppercase) directory that may confuse the build.
steps: In LlamaCppEngineImpl.kt, remove the fallbackInit() method and the mockGenerate() method entirely.

In the initialize() method, when LlamaCppJniBridge.nativeLoaded is false, return Result.failure() with a clear error message like 'Native inference engine not available on this device. The required native libraries (libllama.so) could not be loaded for this device architecture.'
In the initialize() method, when catching UnsatisfiedLinkError, return Result.failure() with a descriptive error instead of falling back to mock.
Remove the useNative flag since there is no longer a mock path. The engine either initializes successfully (native) or fails.
In the generate() method, remove the if/else branching between native and mock. Just call nativeGenerate() directly. If not initialized, throw IllegalStateException as before.

Remove the broken symlinks directory app/src/main/JniLibs/ (uppercase J) which contains broken symlinks (libggml.so -> libggml.so.0 which does not exist). The real libraries are in app/src/main/jniLibs/ (lowercase). The broken symlinks may cause packaging conflicts.
Ensure the ChatViewModel handles the Result.failure case properly - it already shows error in the UI via uiState.error, so the error message from LlamaCppEngineImpl will surface to the user.
acceptance_criteria: LlamaCppEngineImpl no longer contains any mock/fallback code
When native libraries cannot be loaded, initialize() returns a clear failure result
The failure message is user-friendly and explains what is wrong
The broken JniLibs (uppercase) directory with symlinks is removed from git
Code compiles correctly (no references to removed methods)
verification: Read LlamaCppEngineImpl.kt and confirm no mock code remains
Verify the error messages are clear and user-friendly
Verify the JniLibs directory is removed
Check that all references are consistent (no calls to removed methods)
findings: Successfully removed all mock/fallback inference code from LlamaCppEngineImpl.kt. Removed fallbackInit(), mockGenerate(), and useNative flag. The engine now fails clearly with a user-friendly error message when native libraries cannot be loaded, instead of silently falling back to mock output. Also removed app/src/main/JniLibs/ (uppercase J) directory containing 4 broken symlinks (libggml.so -> libggml.so.0, libllama.so -> libllama.so.0 for both arm64-v8a and x86_64) that pointed to nonexistent targets. The real native libraries remain in app/src/main/jniLibs/ (lowercase). Cannot run Gradle build due to INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network but code was verified for Kotlin syntax correctness and no dangling references.
